mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly lower the discount rate for equity valuations, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show no statistical significance or negative mean returns following rate cuts over a 5-year walk-forward test.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.4 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions that trigger immediate risk-off positioning, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in > 65% of triggered instances, or the signal loses predictive power when unemployment rises during recessionary periods.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts indicates synchronized consumer demand growth that supports corporate earnings revisions, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to outperform a baseline model when either series declines, or 60-day forward returns are negative in > 55% of co-expansion periods.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production during periods of low inflation reflects healthy supply-side capacity utilization that boosts equity valuations, yielding positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The mechanism reverses direction when CPIAUCSL exceeds 4.0%, or 20-day returns show no positive correlation with INDPRO growth in high-inflation environments.

mechanism_narrative: When core inflation exceeds 3.5% while the federal funds rate remains below 2.0%, markets price in aggressive future tightening, causing immediate negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX generates positive 5-day returns in > 60% of threshold-crossing events, or the inflation-rate gap loses predictive significance in a multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between rising total employment and falling unemployment indicates labor market tightening that compresses wage growth expectations and supports equity multiples, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The divergence signal yields negative 60-day returns when wage growth accelerates beyond 5%, or the interaction term loses statistical significance in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly growth in real GDP directly correlates with corporate revenue expansion, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days as earnings expectations adjust upward.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show zero or negative correlation with GDPC1 growth rates over a 10-year rolling window, or Rank IC falls below 0.02.

mechanism_narrative: Sharp contractions in industrial production during early recession phases trigger liquidity crunches and margin calls, driving negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: The signal fails to predict negative returns when industrial production declines during late-cycle expansions, or 5-day returns remain positive in > 50% of contraction events.
